posture: Watch (ran clean) — no fresh acute trigger surfaced across the free sources in the last 24 hours, extending the quiet weekend cycle into Sunday. No new political, delivery, supply-chain, market, safety or cyber signal cleared the NSW-REZ relevance bar today, so no items are logged. Standing exposures are unchanged and carried forward: the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration remains unconfirmed as released (now several days past its end-June target — the agency's access-scheme page still reads 'Updated 27 February 2026' and repeats the end-June expectation); EnergyCo's New England corridor field-investigation campaign runs July–September across contested landholdings while the Walcha/High Country opposition and the Coalition's pledge to cap the zone and scrap the Bayswater 500kV link stay live; and the DPHI investigation into ACEREZ's destruction of an Aboriginal rock shelter on the Central-West Orana build is still open.
summary: A full scan of the free source register returned no new NSW-REZ-relevant development in the last 24 hours. Every thread that surfaced today (the New England access-scheme timing, the 1km transmission-corridor refinement announced 1 May, the July–September field-investigation program, the Walcha 22,000-signature petition and the Coalition's cap-and-scrap pledge, the ACEREZ heritage investigation, and the AEMO Services Tender 9 long-duration storage bids that closed 6 July) is either a prior-digest entry with no fresh movement or dated outside the 24-hour window, and is deduplicated out per the writing rules. Today is therefore published with empty sections as a continuity / 'ran clean' marker. The most material standing signal remains procedural: EnergyCo's target of releasing the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration by end June has still not been confirmed as exhibited on the agency's access-scheme page, keeping a low-grade schedule-slippage watch live ahead of the end-September ministerial declaration and the March 2027 state-election backdrop.
